mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment indicates expanding labor income that feeds into aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when PAYEMS increased, or the Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and 20-day returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 45% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the trigger fails to produce a significant negative mean return (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in industrial production and increase in the unemployment rate signals a coordinated contraction in real output and labor demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 50% of joint-signal occurrences, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (incremental R-squared < 0.01) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in quarterly real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion signals diminishing demand momentum that translates into downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns following a GDP growth deceleration are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) during late-cycle periods, or the signal Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected acceleration in the consumer price index signals higher inflation persistence that prompts an upward repricing of near-term discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.5 index points
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns following a CPI acceleration trigger are negative in fewer than 45% of events, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates combined with declining retail sales signal a monetary policy overhang that is actively constraining consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high FEDFUNDS and falling RSAFS does not produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals a weakening labor market that reduces household income expectations and corporate profitability, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in fewer than 45% of months when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the mean 5-day return conditional on this trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns following a month-over-month decline in HOUST are negative in fewer than 50% of occurrences, or the Rank IC between HOUST changes and 20-day returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.
